client.h(3) Library Functions Manual client.h(3)

NAME

client.h - Per-client state management and lifecycle orchestration.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct client_manager_t
Global client manager structure for server-side client coordination.

Typedefs


typedef struct server_context_t server_context_t

Functions


client_info_t * add_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, socket_t socket, const char *client_ip, int port)
client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, const char *client_ip, bool start_threads)
Register a WebRTC client with the server. int start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t *server_ctx, const char *client_id)
Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization. int remove_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, const char *client_id)
client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *client_id)
Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t socket)
Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. void cleanup_client_media_buffers (client_info_t *client)
void cleanup_client_packet_queues (client_info_t *client)
void * client_receive_thread (void *arg)
void stop_client_threads (client_info_t *client)
int process_encrypted_packet (client_info_t *client, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len, uint32_t *sender_id)
void process_decrypted_packet (client_info_t *client, packet_type_t type, void *data, size_t len)
void initialize_client_info (client_info_t *client)

Variables


client_manager_t g_client_manager
Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock
Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.

Detailed Description

Per-client state management and lifecycle orchestration.

This header provides server-specific client management functions. The client_info_t structure and network logging macros are defined in lib/network/client.h.

Definition in file src/server/client.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct server_context_t server_context_t

Definition at line 16 of file src/server/client.h.

Function Documentation

client_info_t * add_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, socket_t socket, const char * client_ip, int port)

Definition at line 614 of file src/server/client.c.

614                                                                                                           {
615   log_info("[TCP_ADD_CLIENT] ENTER: client_ip=%s, port=%d", client_ip, port);
616   // Find empty slot WITHOUT holding the global lock
617   // We'll re-verify under lock after allocations complete
618   int slot = -1;
619   int existing_count = 0;
620   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
621     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
622       slot = i; // Take first available slot
623     }
624     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
625       existing_count++;
626     }
627   }
628 
629   // Quick pre-check before expensive allocations
630   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_CHECK: existing_count=%d, max_clients=%d, slot=%d", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients),
631            slot);
632   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients) || slot == -1) {
633     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Maximum client limit reached\n";
634     ssize_t send_result = socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
635     if (send_result < 0) {
636       log_warn("Failed to send rejection message to client: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
637     }
638     return NULL;
639   }
640 
641   // NOW acquire the lock early for generating unique client name
642   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRE_START");
643   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
644   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED");
645 
646   // Generate unique client ID (noun only, without counter or port)
647   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
648   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_START");
649   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
650     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
651     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID");
652     return NULL;
653   }
654   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_DONE: id=%s", new_client_id);
655 
656   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASE_START");
657   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
658   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASED");
659 
660   // DO EXPENSIVE ALLOCATIONS OUTSIDE THE LOCK
661   // This prevents blocking frame processing during client initialization
662   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_START: About to create video frame buffer for client_id=%s", new_client_id);
663   video_frame_buffer_t *incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
664   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_DONE: Video buffer creation returned %p", (void *)incoming_video_buffer);
665   if (!incoming_video_buffer) {
666     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
667     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
668     return NULL;
669   }
670 
671   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_DONE: About to create audio buffer");
672   audio_ring_buffer_t *incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
673   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_DONE: Audio buffer created");
674   if (!incoming_audio_buffer) {
675     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
676     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
677     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
678     return NULL;
679   }
680 
681   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_START: Creating audio queue");
682   packet_queue_t *audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
683   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_DONE");
684   if (!audio_queue) {
685     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for client");
686     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
687     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
688     return NULL;
689   }
690 
691   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_START: Creating outgoing video buffer");
692   video_frame_buffer_t *outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
693   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_DONE");
694   if (!outgoing_video_buffer) {
695     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for client");
696     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
697     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
698     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
699     return NULL;
700   }
701 
702   void *send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, 64, void *);
703   if (!send_buffer) {
704     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
705     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
706     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
707     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
708     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
709     return NULL;
710   }
711 
712   // NOW acquire the lock for the critical section: slot assignment + registration
713   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_START: About to acquire client manager write lock");
714   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
715   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED: Client manager lock acquired");
716   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_RECHECK: About to check if slot is still available");
717 
718   // Re-check slot availability under lock (another thread might have taken it)
719   if (g_client_manager.clients[slot].client_id[0] != '\0') {
720     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
721     SAFE_FREE(send_buffer);
722     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
723     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
724     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
725     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
726 
727     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Slot reassigned, try again\n";
728     socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
729     return NULL;
730   }
731 
732   // Now we have exclusive access to the slot - do the actual registration
733   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
734   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
735 
736   // Initialize codec capabilities to zero (no codecs supported until CLIENT_CAPABILITIES received)
737   client->codec_capabilities_video = 0;
738   client->codec_capabilities_audio = 0;
739 
740   client->socket = socket;
741   client->is_tcp_client = true;
742   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
743   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
744   client->port = port;
745   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
746   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for cleanup
747   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
748   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0);
749   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);
750   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
751 
752   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
753   client->crypto_initialized = false;
754 
755   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
756   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
757   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
758 
759   // Assign pre-allocated buffers
760   client->incoming_video_buffer = incoming_video_buffer;
761   client->incoming_audio_buffer = incoming_audio_buffer;
762   client->audio_queue = audio_queue;
763   client->outgoing_video_buffer = outgoing_video_buffer;
764   client->send_buffer = send_buffer;
765   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
766 
767   // Generate unique noun-based client name with transport type and port
768   // Note: We're holding the write lock here, so it's safe to iterate existing clients
769   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME");
770   if (generate_client_name(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), g_client_manager.clients_by_id, port,
771                            true /* is_tcp */) != 0) {
772     // Fallback to numeric name if generation fails
773     safe_snprintf(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), "client_%u (tcp:%d)", new_client_id, port);
774   }
775   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME: display_name=%s", client->display_name);
776 
777   // Register client with named debug system using the generated name
778   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT(client, client->display_name, NULL);
779 
780   log_info("Added new client %s from %s:%d (socket=%d, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, port, socket, slot);
781   log_debug("Client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d, socket=%d", new_client_id, slot, socket);
782 
783   // Register socket with tcp_server
784   asciichat_error_t reg_result = tcp_server_add_client(server_ctx->tcp_server, socket, client);
785   if (reg_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
786     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to register client socket with tcp_server");
787     log_error("Failed to register client %s socket with tcp_server", new_client_id);
788     // Don't unlock here - error_cleanup will do it
789     goto error_cleanup;
790   }
791 
792   g_client_manager.client_count++;
793   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added client_id=%s to slot %d)", g_client_manager.client_count,
794             new_client_id, slot);
795 
796   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
797   log_debug("Added client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
798 
799   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
800 
801   // Configure socket OUTSIDE lock
802   configure_client_socket(socket, new_client_id);
803 
804   // Initialize mutexes OUTSIDE lock BEFORE registering them
805   // register_client_info_atomics needs these mutexes to be initialized first
806   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
807     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
808     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
809     return NULL;
810   }
811 
812   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
813     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
814     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
815     return NULL;
816   }
817 
818   // Register all atomic fields for debug tracking - DISABLED to prevent deadlock
819   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() call
820   // NAMED_REGISTER which tries to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock
821   // The debug_sync thread holds READ lock while printing, causing reader-writer deadlock
822   // These are debug-only features not critical for functionality
823   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
824   // register_client_info_atomics(client);
825   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
826 
827   // Audio buffer atomics registration disabled - same deadlock reason
828   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_START: client=%p", (void *)client);
829   // if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
830   //   audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics(client->incoming_audio_buffer, new_client_id);
831   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_DONE");
832   // } else {
833   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_SKIPPED: incoming_audio_buffer is NULL");
834   // }
835 
836   // Register with audio mixer OUTSIDE lock
837   // CRITICAL: Do this BEFORE crypto handshake to avoid deadlock with mixer thread
838   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_START: g_audio_mixer=%p, client->incoming_audio_buffer=%p", (void *)g_audio_mixer,
839            (void *)client->incoming_audio_buffer);
840   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
841     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_CALLING: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
842     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
843       log_warn("Failed to add client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
844     } else {
845       log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SUCCESS: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
846 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
847       log_debug("Added client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
848 #endif
849     }
850   } else {
851     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SKIPPED: conditions not met");
852   }
853   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_DONE");
854 
855   // Perform crypto handshake before starting threads.
856   // This ensures the handshake uses the socket directly without interference from receive thread.
857   log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_START: About to call server_crypto_init()");
858   if (server_crypto_init() == 0) {
859     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_DONE: server_crypto_init() returned 0");
860     // Set timeout for crypto handshake to prevent indefinite blocking
861     // This prevents clients from connecting but never completing the handshake
862     const uint64_t HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS = 30ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
863     asciichat_error_t timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS);
864     if (timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
865       log_warn("Failed to set handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
866                asciichat_error_string(timeout_result));
867       // Continue anyway - timeout is a safety feature, not critical
868     }
869 
870     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_START: About to call server_crypto_handshake()");
871     int crypto_result = server_crypto_handshake(client);
872     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DONE: result=%d", crypto_result);
873     if (crypto_result != 0) {
874       log_error("Crypto handshake failed for client %s: %s", new_client_id, network_error_string());
875       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
876         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
877       }
878       return NULL;
879     }
880 
881     // Clear socket timeout after handshake completes successfully
882     // This allows normal operation without timeouts on data transfer
883     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CLEAR_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_START");
884     asciichat_error_t clear_timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, 0);
885     if (clear_timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
886       log_warn("Failed to clear handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
887                asciichat_error_string(clear_timeout_result));
888       // Continue anyway - we can still communicate even with timeout set
889     }
890 
891     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", new_client_id);
892 
893     // Create ACIP transport for protocol-agnostic packet sending
894     // The transport wraps the socket with encryption context from the handshake
895     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(new_client_id);
896     client->transport = acip_tcp_transport_create(new_client_id, socket, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
897     if (!client->transport) {
898       log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for client %s", new_client_id);
899       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
900         log_error("Failed to remove client after transport creation failure");
901       }
902       return NULL;
903     }
904     log_debug("Created ACIP transport for client %s with crypto context", new_client_id);
905 
906     // After handshake completes, the client immediately sends PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
907     // We must read and process this packet BEFORE starting the receive thread to avoid a race condition
908     // where the packet arrives but no thread is listening for it.
909     //
910     // SPECIAL CASE: If client used --no-encrypt, we already received this packet during the handshake
911     // attempt and stored it in pending_packet_*. Use that instead of receiving a new one.
912     packet_envelope_t envelope;
913     bool used_pending_packet = false;
914 
915     if (client->pending_packet_payload) {
916       // Client used --no-encrypt mode - use the packet we already received
917       log_info("Client %s using --no-encrypt mode - processing pending packet type %u", client->client_id,
918                client->pending_packet_type);
919       envelope.type = client->pending_packet_type;
920       envelope.data = client->pending_packet_payload;
921       envelope.len = client->pending_packet_length;
922       envelope.allocated_buffer = client->pending_packet_payload; // Will be freed below
923       envelope.allocated_size = client->pending_packet_length;
924       used_pending_packet = true;
925 
926       // Clear pending packet fields
927       client->pending_packet_type = 0;
928       client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
929       client->pending_packet_length = 0;
930     } else {
931       // Normal encrypted mode - receive capabilities packet
932       log_debug("Waiting for initial capabilities packet from client %s", client->client_id);
933 
934       // Protect crypto context access with client state mutex
935       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
936       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(client->client_id);
937 
938       // Use per-client crypto state to determine enforcement
939       // At this point, handshake is complete, so crypto_initialized=true and handshake is ready
940       bool enforce_encryption = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
941                                 crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
942 
943       packet_recv_result_t result = receive_packet_secure(socket, (void *)crypto_ctx, enforce_encryption, &envelope);
944       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
945 
946       if (result != PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS) {
947         log_error("Failed to receive initial capabilities packet from client %s: result=%d", client->client_id, result);
948         if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
949           buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
950         }
951         if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
952           log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
953         }
954         return NULL;
955       }
956     }
957 
958     if (envelope.type != PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES) {
959       log_error("Expected PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES but got packet type %d from client %s", envelope.type,
960                 client->client_id);
961       if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
962         buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
963       }
964       if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
965         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
966       }
967       return NULL;
968     }
969 
970     // Process the capabilities packet directly
971     log_debug("Processing initial capabilities packet from client %s (from %s)", client->client_id,
972               used_pending_packet ? "pending packet" : "network");
973     handle_client_capabilities_packet(client, envelope.data, envelope.len);
974 
975     // Free the packet data
976     if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
977       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
978     }
979     log_debug("Successfully received and processed initial capabilities for client %s", client->client_id);
980   }
981 
982   // Start all client threads in the correct order (unified path for TCP and WebRTC)
983   // This creates: receive thread -> render threads -> send thread
984   // The render threads MUST be created before send thread to avoid the race condition
985   // where send thread reads empty frames before render thread generates the first real frame
986   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
987   if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, true) != 0) {
988     log_error("Failed to start threads for TCP client %s", client_id_snapshot);
989     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
990     remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot);
991     return NULL;
992   }
993   log_debug("Successfully created render threads for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
994 
995   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
996   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
997     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, socket, client_ip, port);
998     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
999       log_warn("Failed to register client %s with session_host", client_id_snapshot);
1000     } else {
1001       log_debug("Client %s registered with session_host as %u", client_id_snapshot, client->session_client_id);
1002     }
1003   }
1004 
1005   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1006   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1007   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_START: About to broadcast to all clients");
1008   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1009   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_DONE");
1010 
1011   log_info("[TCP_DBG] ADD_CLIENT_RETURNING: client_id=%s, client=%p", client->client_id, (void *)client);
1012   return client;
1013 
1014 error_cleanup:
1015   // Clean up all partially allocated resources
1016   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1017   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1018   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1019   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1020   return NULL;
1021 }

    client_info::last_rendered_grid_sources,
    client_info::last_sent_grid_sources, packet_envelope_t::len,
    log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_info, log_warn, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN,
    MAX_CLIENTS, MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, mixer_add_source(),
    mutex_init(), mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT, network_error_string(),
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, client_info::outgoing_video_buffer,
    packet_queue_create_with_pools(), packet_queue_destroy(),
    PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS, PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES,
    client_info::pending_packet_length,
    client_info::pending_packet_payload,
    client_info::pending_packet_type, client_info::port,
    receive_packet_secure(), remove_client(),
    rwlock_wrlock, rwlock_wrunlock, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED, safe_snprintf(), SAFE_STRERROR,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, client_info::send_buffer,
    client_info::send_buffer_size, client_info::send_mutex,
    server_crypto_handshake(), server_crypto_init(),
    client_info::server_ctx, client_info::session_client_id,
    server_context_t::session_host, session_host_add_client(),
    SET_ERRNO, set_socket_timeout(),
    client_info::shutting_down, client_info::socket,
    socket_send(), server_context_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), client_info::transport,
    packet_envelope_t::type, video_frame_buffer_create(), and
    video_frame_buffer_destroy().






client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t
  * server_ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, const char * client_ip,
  bool start_threads)


Register a WebRTC client with the server. Registers a client that
    connected via WebRTC data channel instead of TCP socket. This function
    reuses most of add_client() logic but skips:


    
  
  • Crypto handshake (already done via ACDS signaling)
    • 
  
  • Socket-specific configuration
    • 
  
  • TCP thread pool registration
    • 



DIFFERENCES FROM add_client():


    
  
  • Takes an already-created acip_transport_t* instead of socket
    • 
  
  • No crypto handshake (WebRTC signaling handled authentication)
    • 
  
  • No socket configuration (WebRTC handles buffering)
    • 
  
  • Uses generic thread spawning instead of tcp_server thread pool
    • 



Parameters


server_ctx Server context


transport WebRTC transport (already created and connected)


client_ip Client IP address for logging (may be empty for P2P)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t on success, NULL on
  failure


Note


The transport must be fully initialized and ready to
  send/receive



Client capabilities are still expected as first packet




Definition at line 1046 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1047                                                      {
1048   if (!server_ctx || !transport || !client_ip) {
1049     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters to add_webrtc_client");
1050     return NULL;
1051   }
1052 
1053   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1054 
1055   // Find empty slot - this is the authoritative check
1056   int slot = -1;
1057   int existing_count = 0;
1058   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1059     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
1060       slot = i; // Take first available slot
1061     }
1062     // Count only active clients
1063     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1064       existing_count++;
1065     }
1066   }
1067 
1068   // Check if we've hit the configured max-clients limit (not the array size)
1069   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients)) {
1070     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1071     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count,
1072               GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1073     log_error("Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1074     return NULL;
1075   }
1076 
1077   if (slot == -1) {
1078     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1079     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1080     log_error("No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1081     return NULL;
1082   }
1083 
1084   // Update client_count to match actual count before adding new client
1085   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count;
1086 
1087   // Generate unique client ID for WebRTC client (noun only, without counter or port)
1088   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
1089   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
1090     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1091     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID for WebRTC client");
1092     return NULL;
1093   }
1094 
1095   // Initialize client
1096   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
1097 
1098   // Free any existing buffers from previous client in this slot
1099   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1100     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1101     client->incoming_video_buffer = NULL;
1102   }
1103   if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1104     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
1105     client->outgoing_video_buffer = NULL;
1106   }
1107   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1108     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_audio_buffer);
1109     client->incoming_audio_buffer = NULL;
1110   }
1111   if (client->send_buffer) {
1112     SAFE_FREE(client->send_buffer);
1113     client->send_buffer = NULL;
1114   }
1115 
1116   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1117 
1118   // Set up WebRTC-specific fields
1119   client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // WebRTC has no traditional socket
1120   client->is_tcp_client = false;         // WebRTC client - threads managed directly
1121   client->transport = transport;         // Use provided transport
1122   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
1123   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
1124   client->port = 0; // WebRTC doesn't use port numbers
1125   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
1126   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for receive thread cleanup
1127   log_info("Added new WebRTC client %s from %s (transport=%p, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, transport, slot);
1128   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
1129   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0); // Render thread updates this
1130   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);     // Send thread updates this
1131   log_debug("WebRTC client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d", client->client_id, slot);
1132   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
1133 
1134   // Initialize crypto context for this client
1135   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
1136   log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Setting crypto_initialized=false (will be set to true after handshake completes)");
1137   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized = true here! The handshake must complete first.
1138   // For WebSocket clients: websocket_client_handler will perform the handshake
1139   // For WebRTC clients: ACDS signaling may have already done crypto (future enhancement)
1140   client->crypto_initialized = false;
1141 
1142   // Initialize pending packet storage (unused for WebRTC, but keep for consistency)
1143   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
1144   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
1145   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
1146 
1147   // Use noun-based client name for display_name as well
1148   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, new_client_id, sizeof(client->display_name) - 1);
1149 
1150   // Create individual video buffer for this client using modern double-buffering
1151   client->incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1152   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1153     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1154     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1155     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1156   }
1157 
1158   // Create individual audio buffer for this client
1159   client->incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1160   if (!client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1161     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1162     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1163     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1164   }
1165 
1166   // Create packet queues for outgoing data
1167   client->audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
1168   if (!client->audio_queue) {
1169     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for WebRTC client");
1170     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1171   }
1172 
1173   // Create packet queue for async dispatch: received packets waiting to be processed
1174   // Capacity of 100 packets allows buffering of ~10-50 frames (depending on fragmentation)
1175   client->received_packet_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(100, 500, false);
1176   if (!client->received_packet_queue) {
1177     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create received packet queue for client");
1178     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1179   }
1180 
1181   // Create outgoing video buffer for ASCII frames (double buffered, no dropping)
1182   client->outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1183   if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1184     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for WebRTC client");
1185     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1186   }
1187 
1188   // Pre-allocate send buffer to avoid malloc/free in send thread (prevents deadlocks)
1189   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE; // 2MB should handle largest frames
1190   client->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(client->send_buffer_size, 64, void *);
1191   if (!client->send_buffer) {
1192     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1193     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1194   }
1195 
1196   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count + 1; // We just added a client
1197   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added WebRTC client_id=%s to slot %d)",
1198             g_client_manager.client_count, new_client_id, slot);
1199 
1200   // Add client to uthash table for O(1) lookup
1201   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
1202   log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
1203 
1204   // Release the write lock IMMEDIATELY after adding to hash table
1205   // All subsequent operations (mutex init, mixer registration) don't need global client manager lock
1206   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1207 
1208   // Register this client's audio buffer with the mixer
1209   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1210     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
1211       log_warn("Failed to add WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1212     } else {
1213 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1214       log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1215 #endif
1216     }
1217   }
1218 
1219   // Initialize mutexes BEFORE creating any threads to prevent race conditions
1220   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
1221     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1222     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1223     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
1224     return NULL;
1225   }
1226 
1227   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent socket writes
1228   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
1229     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1230     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1231     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1232     return NULL;
1233   }
1234 
1235   // Initialize condition variable for dispatch thread wake-up
1236   if (cond_init(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, "dispatch_queue_cond") != 0) {
1237     log_error("Failed to initialize dispatch queue condition variable for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1238     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1239     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1240     return NULL;
1241   }
1242 
1243   // For WebRTC clients, the capabilities packet will be received by the receive thread
1244   // when it starts. Unlike TCP clients where we handle it synchronously in add_client(),
1245   // WebRTC uses the transport abstraction which handles packet reception automatically.
1246   log_debug("WebRTC client %s initialized - receive thread will process capabilities", new_client_id);
1247 
1248   // Conditionally start threads based on caller preference
1249   // WebSocket handler passes start_threads=false to defer thread startup until after crypto init
1250   // This ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto context is ready
1251   if (start_threads) {
1252     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Starting client threads (receive, render) for client %s...", new_client_id);
1253     if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false) != 0) {
1254       log_error("Failed to start threads for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1255       return NULL;
1256     }
1257     log_debug("Created receive thread for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1258     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Receive thread started - thread will now be processing packets", new_client_id);
1259   } else {
1260     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Deferring thread startup for client %s (caller will start after crypto init)",
1261               new_client_id);
1262   }
1263 
1264   // Send initial server state to the new client
1265   if (send_server_state_to_client(client) != 0) {
1266     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1267   } else {
1268 #ifdef DEBUG_NETWORK
1269     log_info("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1270 #endif
1271   }
1272 
1273   // Send initial server state via ACIP transport
1274   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1275   uint32_t connected_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
1276   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1277 
1278   server_state_packet_t state;
1279   state.connected_client_count = connected_count;
1280   state.active_client_count = 0; // Will be updated by broadcast thread
1281   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1282 
1283   // Convert to network byte order
1284   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1285   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1286   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1287   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1288 
1289   asciichat_error_t packet_send_result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1290   if (packet_send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1291     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %s", new_client_id,
1292              asciichat_error_string(packet_send_result));
1293   } else {
1294     log_debug("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %u connected clients", new_client_id,
1295               state.connected_client_count);
1296   }
1297 
1298   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
1299   // WebRTC clients use INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE since they don't have a TCP socket
1300   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
1301     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_ip, 0);
1302     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1303       log_warn("Failed to register WebRTC client %s with session_host", new_client_id);
1304     } else {
1305       log_debug("WebRTC client %s registered with session_host as %u", new_client_id, client->session_client_id);
1306     }
1307   }
1308 
1309   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1310   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1311   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1312 
1313   return client;
1314 
1315 error_cleanup_webrtc:
1316   // Clean up all partially allocated resources for WebRTC client
1317   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1318   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1319   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1320   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1321   return NULL;
1322 }

void
  * client_receive_thread (void * arg)


Definition at line 1785 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1785                                        {
1786   // Log thread startup
1787   log_debug("RECV_THREAD: Thread function entered, arg=%p", arg);
1788 
1789   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1790 
1791   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
1792   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
1793   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
1794   if (!client) {
1795     log_error("Invalid client info in receive thread (NULL pointer)");
1796     return NULL;
1797   }
1798 
1799   // Save this thread's ID so remove_client() can detect self-joins
1800   client->receive_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1801 
1802   log_debug("RECV_THREAD_DEBUG: Thread started, client=%p, client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d", (void *)client, client->client_id,
1803             client->is_tcp_client);
1804 
1805   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested)) {
1806     log_debug("Receive thread for client %s exiting before start (protocol disconnect requested)", client->client_id);
1807     return NULL;
1808   }
1809 
1810   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
1811   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
1812   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1813     log_debug("Receive thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
1814     return NULL;
1815   }
1816 
1817   // Additional validation: check socket is valid
1818   // For TCP clients, validate socket. WebRTC clients use DataChannel (no socket)
1819   if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1820     log_error("Invalid client socket in receive thread");
1821     return NULL;
1822   }
1823 
1824   // NOTE: Atomic registration disabled
1825   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() attempt
1826   // to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock, which conflicts with debug_sync
1827   // thread holding READ lock during printing, causing deadlock.
1828   // These are debug-only features, not critical for functionality.
1829   // The atomics work fine without being registered in the debug system.
1830 
1831   // Enable thread cancellation for clean shutdown
1832   // Thread cancellation not available in platform abstraction
1833   // Threads should exit when g_should_exit is set
1834 
1835   log_debug("Started receive thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
1836 
1837   // Main receive loop - processes packets from transport
1838   // For TCP clients: receives from socket
1839   // For WebRTC clients: receives from transport ringbuffer (via ACDS signaling)
1840 
1841   log_info("RECV_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d, transport=%p, active=%d", client->client_id,
1842            client->is_tcp_client, (void *)client->transport, atomic_load_bool(&client->active));
1843 
1844   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active)) {
1845     // For TCP clients, check socket validity
1846     // For WebRTC clients, continue even if no socket (transport handles everything)
1847     if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1848       log_debug("TCP client %s has invalid socket, exiting receive thread", client->client_id);
1849       break;
1850     }
1851 
1852     // Check client_id is still valid before accessing transport.
1853     // This prevents accessing freed memory if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct.
1854     if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1855       log_debug("Client client_id reset, exiting receive thread");
1856       break;
1857     }
1858 
1859     // Receive packet (without dispatching) - decouple from dispatch for async processing
1860     // For WebRTC clients with async dispatch, we queue packets instead of processing immediately
1861     // This prevents backpressure on the network socket
1862 
1863     if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1864       // TCP clients: use original synchronous dispatch
1865       asciichat_error_t acip_result =
1866           acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(client->transport, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1867 
1868       // Check if shutdown was requested during the network call
1869       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
1870         log_debug("RECV_EXIT: Server shutdown requested, breaking loop");
1871         break;
1872       }
1873 
1874       // Handle receive errors
1875       if (acip_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1876         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1877         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1878           log_error("🔴 ACIP error for client %s: code=%u msg=%s", client->client_id, err_ctx.code,
1879                     err_ctx.context_message);
1880           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1881             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1882             break;
1883           } else if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
1884             log_error_client(
1885                 client, "SECURITY VIOLATION: Unencrypted packet when encryption required - terminating connection");
1886             atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1887             break;
1888           }
1889         }
1890         log_warn("ACIP error for TCP client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1891                  asciichat_error_string(acip_result));
1892         break;
1893       }
1894     } else {
1895       // WebRTC/WebSocket clients: async dispatch - receive packet and queue for async processing
1896       void *packet_data = NULL;
1897       void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
1898       size_t packet_len = 0;
1899 
1900       // Snapshot transport pointer to prevent use-after-free
1901       // Transport can be destroyed by WebSocket callback while we're receiving
1902       acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
1903       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1904       if (!client->transport || atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1905         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1906         log_debug("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Transport destroyed or client shutting down, exiting receive loop",
1907                   client->client_id);
1908         break;
1909       }
1910       transport_snapshot = client->transport;
1911       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1912 
1913       log_debug("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: About to call transport->recv() (transport=%p)", client->client_id,
1914                 (void *)transport_snapshot);
1915 
1916       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1917           transport_snapshot->methods->recv(transport_snapshot, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
1918 
1919       // Check error BEFORE logging - logging system may clear thread-local errno
1920       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1921         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1922         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1923           // Check for reassembly timeout (fragments arriving slowly)
1924           // This is NOT a connection failure - safe to retry
1925           if ((err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) && err_ctx.context_message &&
1926               strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "reassembly timeout")) {
1927             // Fragments are arriving slowly - this is normal, retry without disconnecting
1928             log_dev_every(100000, "Client %s: fragment reassembly timeout, retrying in 10ms", client->client_id);
1929             platform_sleep_ms(10); // Sleep 10ms to allow fragments to arrive
1930             continue;              // Retry without disconnecting
1931           }
1932 
1933           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1934             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1935             break;
1936           }
1937         }
1938         log_warn("Receive failed for WebRTC client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1939                  asciichat_error_string(recv_result));
1940         break;
1941       }
1942 
1943       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: transport->recv() returned result=%d, packet_len=%zu, allocated_buffer=%p",
1944                client->client_id, recv_result, packet_len, allocated_buffer);
1945 
1946       // Validate received packet before queueing
1947       if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1948         log_warn("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Received packet too small (%zu < %zu), dropping", client->client_id, packet_len,
1949                  sizeof(packet_header_t));
1950         if (allocated_buffer) {
1951           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1952         }
1953         continue;
1954       }
1955 
1956       // Queue the received packet for async dispatch
1957       // This prevents the receive thread from blocking on dispatch
1958       log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Queuing %zu byte packet for async dispatch", client->client_id, packet_len);
1959 
1960       // Extract packet type from the header to preserve it when queueing
1961       const packet_header_t *pkt_header = (const packet_header_t *)allocated_buffer;
1962       packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(pkt_header->type);
1963       uint32_t payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(pkt_header->length);
1964 
1965       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Packet header: type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1966                payload_len, packet_len);
1967 
1968       // Build a complete packet to queue (header + payload)
1969       // The entire buffer (allocated_buffer) contains the full packet
1970       // copy_data=false because we want to transfer ownership to the queue
1971       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
1972       int enqueue_result = packet_queue_enqueue(client->received_packet_queue, pkt_type, allocated_buffer, packet_len,
1973                                                 client_id_hash, false);
1974 
1975       if (enqueue_result < 0) {
1976         log_error("🔴 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Failed to queue received packet (queue full?) - DROPPING FRAME",
1977                   client->client_id);
1978         if (allocated_buffer) {
1979           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1980         }
1981       } else {
1982         log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Successfully queued packet (type=%d, len=%zu)", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1983                  packet_len);
1984         // Signal dispatch thread to wake up and process the newly queued packet
1985         cond_signal(&client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1986       }
1987     }
1988   }
1989 
1990   // Mark client as inactive and stop all threads
1991   // Must stop render threads when client disconnects.
1992   // OPTIMIZED: Use atomic operations for thread control flags (lock-free)
1993   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
1994   log_debug("Setting active=false in receive_thread_fn (client_id=%s, exiting receive loop)", client_id_snapshot);
1995   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1996   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
1997   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1998   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
1999 
2000   // Call remove_client() to trigger cleanup
2001   // Safe to call from receive thread now: remove_client() detects self-join via thread IDs
2002   // and skips the receive thread join when called from the receive thread itself
2003   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s calling remove_client() for cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2004   server_context_t *server_ctx = (server_context_t *)client->server_ctx;
2005   if (server_ctx) {
2006     if (remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot) != 0) {
2007       log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from receive thread cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2008     }
2009   } else {
2010     log_error("Receive thread for client %s: server_ctx is NULL, cannot call remove_client()", client_id_snapshot);
2011   }
2012 
2013   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s terminated", client_id_snapshot);
2014 
2015   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2016   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2017 
2018   return NULL;
2019 }






References acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(),
    client_info::active, asciichat_errno_destroy(),
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_self(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    client_info::audio_render_thread_running, buffer_pool_free(),
    client_info::client_id, asciichat_error_context_t::code,
    cond_signal(), asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    client_info::dispatch_queue_cond, client_info::display_name,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_NETWORK, g_should_exit,
    HAS_ERRNO, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::is_tcp_client, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_dev_every, log_error,
    log_error_client, log_info, log_warn,
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    packet_queue_enqueue(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    client_info::protocol_disconnect_requested,
    client_info::receive_thread_id,
    client_info::received_packet_queue,
    acip_transport_methods_t::recv, remove_client(),
    client_info::send_mutex, client_info::send_thread_running,
    client_info::server_ctx, client_info::shutting_down,
    client_info::socket, client_info::transport,
    packet_header_t::type, and
    client_info::video_render_thread_running.






client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *
  client_id)


Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. This is the
    primary method for locating clients throughout the server. It uses a hash
    table for constant-time lookups regardless of client count, making it
    suitable for high-performance operations like rendering and stats.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case (hash collision)
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Hash table is internally thread-safe for lookups
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads to find target clients for frame generation
    • 
  
  • Used by protocol handlers to locate clients for packet processing
    • 
  
  • Stats collection for per-client performance monitoring
    • 



Parameters


client_id Unique identifier for the client (0 is
  invalid)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found or invalid ID


Note


Does not require external locking - hash table provides
  thread safety



Returns direct pointer to client struct - caller should use
    snapshot pattern




Definition at line 387 of file
  src/server/client.c.


387                                                         {
388   if (!client_id || client_id[0] == '\0') {
389     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid client ID");
390     return NULL;
391   }
392 
393   // Protect uthash lookup with read lock to prevent concurrent access issues
394   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
395 
396   client_info_t *result = NULL;
397   HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, result);
398 
399   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
400 
401   if (!result) {
402     log_warn("Client not found for ID %s", client_id);
403   }
404 
405   return result;
406 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, log_warn, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    crypto_server_cleanup_client(),
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    crypto_server_is_ready(), and
  start_webrtc_client_threads().






client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t
  socket)


Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. This
    function provides socket-based client lookup, primarily used during
    connection establishment before client IDs are assigned. Less efficient than
    find_client_by_id() but necessary for socket-based operations.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of active clients
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Internally acquires read lock on
    g_client_manager_rwlock
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Connection establishment during add_client() processing
    • 
  
  • Socket error handling and cleanup operations
    • 
  
  • Debugging and diagnostic functions
    • 



Parameters


socket Platform-abstracted socket descriptor to
  search for


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found


Note


Only searches active clients (avoids returning stale
  entries)



Caller should use snapshot pattern when accessing returned client
    data




Definition at line 431 of file
  src/server/client.c.

432   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
433 
434   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
435     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].socket == socket && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
436       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
437       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
438       return client;
439     }
440   }
441 
442   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
443   return NULL;
444 }

3746   }
3747 
3748   // Rate limiting: Check and record packet-specific rate limits
3749   if (g_rate_limiter) {

3751       // Rate limit exceeded - error response already sent by utility function
3752       return;

3758   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {

3768   }

3771     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, handler returned", type);
3772   }

Process an encrypted packet from a client

client Client info structure

2809     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2810     *data = NULL;
2811     return -1;
2812   }
2813 
2814   // Store original allocation size before it gets modified
2815   size_t original_alloc_size = *len;
2816   void *decrypted_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, original_alloc_size);
2817   size_t decrypted_len;
2818   int decrypt_result = crypto_server_decrypt_packet(client->client_id, (const uint8_t *)*data, *len,
2819                                                     (uint8_t *)decrypted_data, original_alloc_size, &decrypted_len);
2820 
2821   if (decrypt_result != 0) {
2822     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process encrypted packet from client %s (result=%d)", client->client_id,
2823               decrypt_result);

2825     buffer_pool_free(NULL, decrypted_data, original_alloc_size);

2828   }
2829 
2830   // Replace encrypted data with decrypted data
2831   // Use original allocation size for freeing the encrypted buffer
2832   buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2833 
2834   *data = decrypted_data;
2835   *len = decrypted_len;
2836 
2837   // Now process the decrypted packet by parsing its header
2838   if (*len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
2839     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Decrypted packet too small for header from client %s", client->client_id);
2840     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2841     *data = NULL;
2842     return -1;
2843   }

2847   *sender_id = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->client_id);
2848 
2849   // Adjust data pointer to skip header

2851   *len -= sizeof(packet_header_t);
2852 
2853   return 0;
2854 }

int
  remove_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char *
  client_id)


Definition at line 1324 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1324                                                                        {
1325   if (!server_ctx) {
1326     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot remove client %s: NULL server_ctx", client_id);
1327     return -1;
1328   }
1329 
1330   // Phase 1: Mark client inactive and prepare for cleanup while holding write lock
1331   client_info_t *target_client = NULL;
1332   char display_name_copy[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
1333   socket_t client_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1334 
1335   log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Attempting to remove client %s", client_id);
1336   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1337 
1338   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1339     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1340     if (strcmp(client->client_id, client_id) == 0 && client->client_id[0] != '\0') {
1341       // Check if already being removed by another thread
1342       // This prevents double-free and use-after-free crashes during concurrent cleanup
1343       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1344         rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1345         log_debug("Client %s already being removed by another thread, skipping", client_id);
1346         return 0; // Return success - removal is in progress
1347       }
1348       // Mark as shutting down and inactive immediately to stop new operations
1349       log_debug("Setting active=false in remove_client (client_id=%s, socket=%d)", client_id, client->socket);
1350       log_info("Removing client %s (socket=%d) - marking inactive and clearing video flags", client_id, client->socket);
1351       atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
1352       atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1353       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
1354       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
1355       target_client = client;
1356 
1357       // Store display name before clearing
1358       SAFE_STRNCPY(display_name_copy, client->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
1359 
1360       // Save socket for tcp_server_stop_client_threads() before closing
1361       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1362       client_socket = client->socket; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1363       // NOTE: Do NOT call socket_shutdown() here - it sends FIN to peer and breaks the connection
1364       // The threads will exit naturally when they check shutting_down/active flags or hit errors
1365       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1366 
1367       // Shutdown packet queues to unblock send thread
1368       if (client->audio_queue) {
1369         packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
1370       }
1371       // Video now uses double buffer, no queue to shutdown
1372 
1373       break;
1374     }
1375   }
1376 
1377   // If client not found, unlock and return
1378   if (!target_client) {
1379     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1380     log_warn("Cannot remove client %s: not found", client_id);
1381     return -1;
1382   }
1383 
1384   // Unregister client from session_host (for discovery mode support)
1385   // NOTE: Client may not be registered if crypto handshake failed before session_host registration
1386   if (server_ctx->session_host && target_client->session_client_id != 0) {
1387     asciichat_error_t session_result =
1388         session_host_remove_client(server_ctx->session_host, target_client->session_client_id);
1389     if (session_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1390       // ERROR_NOT_FOUND (91) is expected if client failed crypto before being registered with session_host
1391       if (session_result == ERROR_NOT_FOUND) {
1392         log_debug("Client %s not found in session_host (likely failed crypto before registration)", client_id);
1393       } else {
1394         log_warn("Failed to unregister client %s from session_host: %s", client_id,
1395                  asciichat_error_string(session_result));
1396       }
1397     } else {
1398       log_debug("Client %s unregistered from session_host", client_id);
1399     }
1400   }
1401 
1402   // Release write lock before joining threads.
1403   // This prevents deadlock with render threads that need read locks.
1404   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1405 
1406   // Phase 2: Stop all client threads
1407   // For TCP clients: use tcp_server thread pool management
1408   // For WebRTC clients: manually join threads (no socket-based thread pool)
1409   // Use is_tcp_client flag, not socket value - socket may already be INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE
1410   // even for TCP clients if it was closed earlier during cleanup.
1411   log_debug("Stopping all threads for client %s (socket %d, is_tcp=%d)", client_id, client_socket,
1412             target_client ? target_client->is_tcp_client : -1);
1413 
1414   if (target_client && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1415     // TCP client: use tcp_server thread pool
1416     // This joins threads in stop_id order: receive(1), render(2), send(3)
1417     // Use saved client_socket for lookup (tcp_server needs original socket as key)
1418     if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1419       asciichat_error_t stop_result = tcp_server_stop_client_threads(server_ctx->tcp_server, client_socket);
1420       if (stop_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1421         log_warn("Failed to stop threads for TCP client %s: error %d", client_id, stop_result);
1422         // Continue with cleanup even if thread stopping failed
1423       }
1424     } else {
1425       log_debug("TCP client %s socket already closed, threads should have already exited", client_id);
1426     }
1427   } else if (target_client) {
1428     // WebRTC client: manually join threads
1429     log_debug("Stopping WebRTC client %s threads (receive and send)", client_id);
1430 
1431     // Join receive thread (but skip if called from the receive thread itself to avoid deadlock)
1432     thread_id_t current_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1433     if (asciichat_thread_equal(current_thread_id, target_client->receive_thread_id)) {
1434       log_debug("remove_client() called from receive thread for client %s, skipping self-join", client_id);
1435     } else {
1436       void *recv_result = NULL;
1437       asciichat_error_t recv_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->receive_thread, &recv_result);
1438       if (recv_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1439         log_warn("Failed to join receive thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, recv_join_result);
1440       } else {
1441         log_debug("Joined receive thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1442       }
1443     }
1444 
1445     // Join dispatch thread (BEFORE destroying packet queues)
1446     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->dispatch_thread)) {
1447       atomic_store_bool(&target_client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
1448       void *dispatch_result = NULL;
1449       asciichat_error_t dispatch_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->dispatch_thread, &dispatch_result);
1450       if (dispatch_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1451         log_warn("Failed to join dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, dispatch_join_result);
1452       } else {
1453         log_debug("Joined dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1454       }
1455     }
1456 
1457     // Join send thread
1458     void *send_result = NULL;
1459     asciichat_error_t send_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->send_thread, &send_result);
1460     if (send_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1461       log_warn("Failed to join send thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, send_join_result);
1462     } else {
1463       log_debug("Joined send thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1464     }
1465     // Note: Render threads still need to be stopped - they're created the same way for both TCP and WebRTC
1466     // For now, render threads are expected to exit when they check g_should_exit and client->active
1467   }
1468 
1469   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
1470   // For WebSocket clients: LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already closed and destroyed the transport
1471   // Trying to destroy it again causes heap-use-after-free since LWS callbacks might still fire
1472   // For TCP clients: transport is ours to clean up
1473   if (target_client && target_client->transport && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1474     acip_transport_destroy(target_client->transport);
1475     target_client->transport = NULL;
1476     log_debug("Destroyed ACIP transport for TCP client %s", client_id);
1477   } else if (target_client && target_client->transport && !target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1478     // WebSocket client - just NULL it out, LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already destroyed it
1479     target_client->transport = NULL;
1480     log_debug("Skipped transport destruction for WebSocket client %s (LWS already destroyed)", client_id);
1481   }
1482 
1483   // Now safe to close the socket (threads are stopped)
1484   if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1485     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Closing socket %d for client %s after thread cleanup", client_socket, client_id);
1486     socket_close(client_socket);
1487   }
1488 
1489   // Phase 3: Clean up resources with write lock
1490   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1491 
1492   // Re-validate target_client pointer after reacquiring lock.
1493   // Another thread might have invalidated the pointer while we had the lock released.
1494   if (target_client) {
1495     // Verify client_id still matches and client is still in shutting_down state
1496     bool still_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&target_client->shutting_down);
1497     if (strcmp(target_client->client_id, client_id) != 0 || !still_shutting_down) {
1498       log_warn("Client %s pointer invalidated during thread cleanup (id=%s, shutting_down=%d)", client_id,
1499                target_client->client_id, still_shutting_down);
1500       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1501       return 0; // Another thread completed the cleanup
1502     }
1503   }
1504 
1505   // Mark socket as closed in client structure
1506   if (target_client && target_client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1507     mutex_lock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1508     target_client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1509     mutex_unlock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1510     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Client %s socket set to INVALID", target_client->client_id);
1511   }
1512 
1513   // Use the dedicated cleanup function to ensure all resources are freed
1514   cleanup_client_all_buffers(target_client);
1515 
1516   // Remove from audio mixer
1517   if (g_audio_mixer) {
1518     mixer_remove_source(g_audio_mixer, client_id);
1519 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1520     log_debug("Removed client %s from audio mixer", client_id);
1521 #endif
1522   }
1523 
1524   // Remove from uthash table
1525   // Verify client is actually in the hash table before deleting.
1526   // Another thread might have already removed it.
1527   if (target_client) {
1528     client_info_t *hash_entry = NULL;
1529     HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client->client_id, hash_entry);
1530     if (hash_entry == target_client) {
1531       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client);
1532       log_debug("Removed client %s from uthash table", client_id);
1533     } else {
1534       log_warn("Client %s already removed from hash table by another thread (found=%p, expected=%p)", client_id,
1535                (void *)hash_entry, (void *)target_client);
1536     }
1537   } else {
1538     log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from hash table (client not found)", client_id);
1539   }
1540 
1541   // Cleanup crypto context for this client
1542   if (target_client->crypto_initialized) {
1543     crypto_handshake_destroy(&target_client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
1544     target_client->crypto_initialized = false;
1545     log_debug("Crypto context cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
1546   }
1547 
1548   // Verify all threads have actually exited before resetting client_id.
1549   // Threads that are still starting (at RtlUserThreadStart) haven't checked client_id yet.
1550   // We must ensure threads are fully joined before zeroing the client struct.
1551   // Use exponential backoff for thread termination verification
1552   int retry_count = 0;
1553   const int max_retries = 5;
1554   while (retry_count < max_retries && (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->send_thread) ||
1555                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->receive_thread) ||
1556                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->video_render_thread) ||
1557                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->audio_render_thread))) {
1558     // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
1559     uint32_t delay_ms = 10 * (1 << retry_count);
1560     log_warn("Client %s: Some threads still appear initialized (attempt %d/%d), waiting %ums", client_id,
1561              retry_count + 1, max_retries, delay_ms);
1562     platform_sleep_us(delay_ms * 1000);
1563     retry_count++;
1564   }
1565 
1566   if (retry_count == max_retries) {
1567     log_error("Client %s: Threads did not terminate after %d retries, proceeding with cleanup anyway", client_id,
1568               max_retries);
1569   }
1570 
1571   // Only reset client_id to 0 AFTER confirming threads are joined
1572   // This prevents threads that are starting from accessing a zeroed client struct
1573   // Reset client_id to NULL before destroying mutexes to prevent race conditions.
1574   // This ensures worker threads can detect shutdown and exit before the mutex is destroyed.
1575   // If we destroy the mutex first, threads might try to access a destroyed mutex.
1576   target_client->client_id[0] = '\0'; // Clear the client_id string
1577 
1578   // Wait for threads to observe the client_id reset
1579   // Use sufficient delay for memory visibility across all CPU cores
1580   platform_sleep_us(5 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 5ms delay for memory barrier propagation
1581 
1582   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
1583   // IMPORTANT: Always destroy these even if threads didn't join properly
1584   // to prevent issues when the slot is reused
1585   mutex_destroy(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1586   mutex_destroy(&target_client->send_mutex);
1587   cond_destroy(&target_client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1588 
1589   // Clear client structure
1590   // NOTE: After memset, the mutex handles are zeroed but the OS resources
1591   // have been released by the destroy calls above
1592   memset(target_client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1593 
1594   // Recalculate client count
1595   int remaining_count = 0;
1596   for (int j = 0; j < MAX_CLIENTS; j++) {
1597     if (g_client_manager.clients[j].client_id[0] != '\0') {
1598       remaining_count++;
1599     }
1600   }
1601   g_client_manager.client_count = remaining_count;
1602 
1603   log_debug("Client removed: client_id=%s (%s) removed, remaining clients: %d", client_id, display_name_copy,
1604             remaining_count);
1605 
1606   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1607 
1608   // Broadcast updated state
1609   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1610 
1611   return 0;
1612 }

int
  start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char
  * client_id)


Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization.
    This is called by WebSocket handler after crypto handshake is initialized.
    It ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto
    context exists.




Parameters


server_ctx Server context


client_id Client ID to start threads for


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

  src/server/client.c.


2668                                                                                      {
2669   if (!server_ctx) {
2670     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Server context is NULL");
2671     return -1;
2672   }
2673 
2674   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
2675   if (!client) {
2676     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client %s not found", client_id);
2677     return -1;
2678   }
2679 
2680   log_debug("Starting threads for WebRTC client %s...", client_id);
2681   return start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false);
2682 }

void
  stop_client_threads (client_info_t * client)


Definition at line 2684 of file
  src/server/client.c.


2684                                                 {
2685   if (!client) {
2686     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
2687     return;
2688   }
2689 
2690   // Signal threads to stop
2691   log_debug("Setting active=false in stop_client_threads (client_id=%s)", client->client_id);
2692   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
2693   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2694 
2695   // Wait for threads to finish
2696   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->send_thread)) {
2697     asciichat_thread_join(&client->send_thread, NULL);
2698   }
2699   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->receive_thread)) {
2700     asciichat_thread_join(&client->receive_thread, NULL);
2701   }
2702   // For async dispatch: stop dispatch thread if running
2703   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->dispatch_thread)) {
2704     atomic_store_bool(&client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
2705     asciichat_thread_join(&client->dispatch_thread, NULL);
2706   }
2707 }

Variable
  Documentation




client_manager_t g_client_manager [extern]


Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. This
    is the primary data structure for managing all connected clients. It serves
    as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and the per-client
    threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 334 of file
  src/server/client.c.






rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock [extern]


Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager. This lock
    enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats
      gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    • 



Definition at line 349 of file
  src/server/client.c.
